title: (No AI) The largest orbital compute cluster is open for business

content: Kepler Communications is flying 40 GPUs in Earth orbit. And its latest customer is Sophia Space.

title: (No AI) Trump officials may be encouraging banks to test Anthropic’s Mythos model

title: (No AI) Sam Altman reportedly targeted in second attack
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![Photo collage of Sam Altman in front of the OpenAI logo.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25431700/STK201_SAM_ALTMAN_CVIRGINIA_A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Sam Altman was seemingly targeted by a second attack on Sunday morning, with two suspects arrested following a shooting at his Russian Hill residence, [_The San Francisco Standard_](https://sfstandard.com/2026/04/12/sam-altman-s-home-targeted-second-attack/) reports. The suspects were arrested and charged with negligent discharge, according to a [police report](https://www.sanfranciscopolice.org/news/sfpd-arrests-suspects-involved-shooting-26-044) on the incident, citing surveillance footage that appears to show a vehicle passenger firing a weapon at Altman's home.

This follows [an earlier attack on Friday](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/910393/openai-sam-altman-house-molotov-cocktail), in which a 20-year-old man was arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at the same property. The investigations for both incidents are still ongoing.

According to San Francisco Police, offers w …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/910890/openai-sam-altman-second-home-attack-shooting)
contentSnippet:
Sam Altman was seemingly targeted by a second attack on Sunday morning, with two suspects arrested following a shooting at his Russian Hill residence, The San Francisco Standard reports. The suspects were arrested and charged with negligent discharge, according to a police report on the incident, citing surveillance footage that appears to show a vehicle passenger firing a weapon at Altman's home.
This follows an earlier attack on Friday, in which a 20-year-old man was arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at the same property. The investigations for both incidents are still ongoing.
According to San Francisco Police, offers w …

title: (No AI) The AI code wars are heating up
author: David Pierce
content:
![An animation of laptops racing with live code being generated on their screens](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/268441_AI_CODING_RACE_CVIRGINIA.gif?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

_This is_ [The Stepback](https://www.theverge.com/the-stepback-newsletter)_,_ _a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the AI coding and vibe-coding booms, [follow David Pierce](https://www.theverge.com/authors/david-pierce)._ The Stepback _arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM ET. Opt in for_ The Stepback [_here_](https://www.theverge.com/newsletters)_._

## How it started

Writing code was a killer app for AI even before anyone was really talking about AI. In the spring of 2021, 18 months before the world knew the word "ChatGPT," Microsoft debuted the very first product of a partnership with a nonprofit called OpenAI: [a tool called GitHub Copilot](https://www.theverge.com/2021/6/29/22555777/github-openai-ai-tool-autocomplete-code) that watched developers as they wrote code and tried to autocomplete snippets and lines for them …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/column/910019/ai-coding-wars-openai-google-anthropic)

creator: Gia Chaudry
title: (No AI) How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors
dc:creator: Gia Chaudry
content: From AI-generated images to restricted satellite data, the systems used to verify what’s real online are struggling to keep up.
